story.asA: developer implementing the Recipe conversion engine
story.iWant: a robust XMP XML parser that extracts all Lightroom CC preset parameters from .xmp files
story.soThat: users can convert Adobe Lightroom CC presets to other formats with 95%+ accuracy
story.tasks: - Analyze 10 sample XMP files to understand structure - Create parameter mapping reference table - Implement Parse() function in `parse.go` - Implement table-driven tests in `xmp_test.go` - Validate all 913 sample files parse successfully - Performance benchmark <30ms achieved - Test coverage ≥90% achieved

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Functional Requirements:**
**FR-1: XMP File Validation** - Verify file is valid XML before parsing - Validate required Adobe XMP namespace declarations present (xmlns:x, xmlns:crs, xmlns:rdf) - Reject files missing camera-raw-settings namespace with clear error message - Handle both sidecar XMP and embedded XMP structures
**FR-2: Core Parameter Extraction (Basic Adjustments)** - Extract crs:Exposure2012 → UniversalRecipe.Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0) - Extract crs:Contrast2012 → UniversalRecipe.Contrast (-100 to +100) - Extract crs:Highlights2012, Shadows2012, Whites2012, Blacks2012 (all -100 to +100)
**FR-3: Color Parameter Extraction** - Extract Saturation, Vibrance, Clarity2012, Sharpness, Temperature, Tint
**FR-4: HSL Color Adjustments Extraction** - Extract HSL adjustments for all 8 colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta) - For each color: Hue, Saturation, Luminance (-100 to +100)
**FR-5: Advanced Features Extraction** - Extract ToneCurve (array of Point{X, Y} coordinates) - Extract Split Toning parameters (Shadow/Highlight Hue and Saturation)
**FR-6: Data Type Handling** - Parse floating-point values correctly (Exposure uses float64) - Parse integer values correctly - Handle quoted strings in XML attributes - Handle missing/optional fields gracefully (use zero values) - Validate all numeric values within expected ranges
**FR-7: Error Handling** - Clear errors for invalid XML, missing namespaces, out-of-range parameters - Wrap all errors in ConversionError with context - Include file position information when possible
**Non-Functional Requirements:**
**NFR-1: Performance** - Parse single XMP file in <30ms
**NFR-2: Test Coverage** - Parse all 913 sample XMP files from testdata/xmp/ without errors - Test coverage ≥90% for xmp package - Table-driven tests following Pattern 7 - Validate extracted parameters within expected ranges
**NFR-3: Code Quality** - Follow Pattern 4: File structure (parse.go, xmp_test.go) - Follow Pattern 5: Error handling with ConversionError wrapper - Follow Pattern 6: Inline validation, fail fast - Use stdlib encoding/xml package only (zero external dependencies) - Code passes gofmt and go vet without warnings

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: XMP format uses XML/RDF structure with Adobe XMP namespaces. Parse() validates namespace declarations, uses encoding/xml Unmarshal to struct, converts string values to appropriate types, maps to UniversalRecipe fields. Must extract 50+ parameters including Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, HSL colors, tone curves, split toning.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: UniversalRecipe struct with 50+ fields: Exposure (float64, -5.0 to +5.0), Contrast (int, -100 to +100), Highlights/Shadows/Whites/Blacks (int, -100 to +100), HSL ColorAdjustment (8 colors × 3 properties), ToneCurve ([]Point), Split Toning parameters. Zero values are meaningful (no adjustment). Extensible via Metadata map.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Parse single XMP file in <30ms target. Use encoding/xml (stdlib), pre-compile regex patterns, avoid unnecessary allocations. Validated via Go benchmarks (go test -bench).

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.section: Pattern 4: File Structure for Format Packages
doc.snippet: Every format package has identical structure: parse.go (Parse function), generate.go (Generate function), {format}_test.go (table-driven tests). Identical structure across formats makes it easy to add new formats.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Always return wrapped ConversionError for conversion failures. Provides Operation, Format, Cause fields. Implements Error() and Unwrap() for type-safe error checking.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Validate inline in parsers, fail fast with descriptive errors. Validate magic bytes FIRST, then file size, then parameter ranges during extraction. Clear error messages aid debugging.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using real sample files from testdata/. Use filepath.Glob to discover all test files, run subtest for each file with t.Run(). Validate recipe fields are within expected ranges.

artifact.reason: Target data structure for XMP parser. Must populate 50+ fields including Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, HSL ColorAdjustments (8 colors), ToneCurve points, Split Toning parameters. Note: package is "models" not "model".
Used for HSL color adjustments (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta). Each color has Hue (-180 to +180), Saturation (-100 to +100), Luminance (-100 to +100).
Used for tone curve array. Input/Output values both 0-255 range. Store as array in UniversalRecipe.PointCurve field.
Reference implementation showing parser pattern: validateFileStructure → extractParameters → validateParameters → buildRecipe. Use same pattern for XMP parser.
Builder pattern for constructing UniversalRecipe. Use builder.WithExposure(), WithContrast(), etc. to set fields, then builder.Build() to validate and return recipe.
Validation functions for parameter ranges. May need to create ValidateXMP* functions for XMP-specific validations (Exposure: -5.0 to +5.0, Contrast: -100 to +100, etc.).

stdlib.usage: Core XML parsing and unmarshaling for XMP file structure
stdlib.reason: Required for parsing XML/RDF structure. Use encoding/xml.Unmarshal with struct tags to map XMP attributes to Go fields.
Used for error wrapping with fmt.Errorf() and string formatting
Convert XML string attributes to float64/int values (e.g., crs:Exposure2012="1.5" → float64)
May be needed for cleaning XML attribute values
May be needed for XML namespace validation and byte manipulation

dependencies.constraint: Zero external dependencies - only Go stdlib packages allowed per NFR-3

story-context.constraints: - Follow Pattern 4: File structure must be internal/formats/xmp/{parse.go, generate.go, xmp_test.go} - Follow Pattern 5: Wrap all errors in ConversionError type (to be created in Story 1-9, or use fmt.Errorf for now) - Follow Pattern 6: Validate inline in parser - fail fast with descriptive errors - Follow Pattern 7: Table-driven tests with filepath.Glob to discover all 913 test files - Use builder pattern: models.NewRecipeBuilder() to construct UniversalRecipe - Package name: internal/models (not internal/model as in tech spec) - Zero external dependencies: Use only stdlib encoding/xml - Must extract ALL 50+ parameters listed in acceptance criteria - Validate namespace declarations before parsing (required: xmlns:x, xmlns:crs, xmlns:rdf) - Handle missing optional fields gracefully (use zero values, no crashes) - Performance target: Parse <30ms (validate with benchmarks)

interface.name: UniversalRecipe Builder
interface.signature: builder := models.NewRecipeBuilder() builder.WithSourceFormat("xmp") builder.WithExposure(exposure) builder.WithContrast(contrast) // ... set all fields recipe, err := builder.Build()

interface.name: XMP struct for encoding/xml

tests.standards:
**Testing Approach (Pattern 7):**
1. **Table-Driven Tests**: Use filepath.Glob("testdata/xmp/**/*.xmp") to discover all 913 sample files. Run each file as a t.Run() subtest with descriptive name.
2. **Test Organization**: - TestParse: Main parsing validation with all 913 sample files - TestParseInvalid*: Error handling tests (invalid XML, missing namespaces, corrupted data) - TestExtractParameters: Unit tests for parameter extraction logic - TestValidateParameters: Edge case validation tests - BenchmarkParse: Performance benchmarks (<30ms target)
3. **Validation Strategy**: - Verify Parse() succeeds for all 913 sample files - Validate SourceFormat == "xmp" - Validate all numeric parameters within expected ranges - Validate HSL colors extracted for all 8 colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta) - Validate tone curve points (if present) are valid - Log warnings for missing optional fields (acceptable)
4. **Coverage Target**: ≥90% code coverage for xmp package verified via `go test -cover`
5. **Performance Testing**: Use `go test -bench=. -benchtime=10s` to validate <30ms parse time per file
tests.locations:
**Test Files:** - internal/formats/xmp/xmp_test.go - All XMP parser tests following Pattern 7
**Test Data:** - testdata/xmp/ - 913 sample XMP files from various Lightroom CC versions - testdata/xmp/basic/ - Simple XMP files with minimal adjustments - testdata/xmp/complex/ - Files with HSL colors, tone curves, split toning - testdata/xmp/edge-cases/ - Invalid files, missing namespaces, corrupted XML
**Test Commands:** - go test ./internal/formats/xmp/ - Run all tests - go test ./internal/formats/xmp/ -v - Verbose output - go test ./internal/formats/xmp/ -cover - Coverage report - go test ./internal/formats/xmp/ -bench=. - Performance benchmarks - go test ./internal/formats/xmp/ -run=TestParse/specific_file.xmp - Run single file test
tests.ideas:
**Test Scenarios (organized by Acceptance Criteria):**
**FR-1: XMP File Validation** - TestParseValidXML: Parse valid XMP with all required namespaces - TestParseInvalidXML: Reject malformed XML with descriptive error - TestParseMissingNamespaces: Reject files missing xmlns:x, xmlns:crs, or xmlns:rdf - TestParseMissingCRSNamespace: Specific error for missing camera-raw-settings namespace - TestParseSidecarXMP: Parse sidecar .xmp file structure - TestParseEmbeddedXMP: Parse embedded XMP structure (if applicable)
**FR-2: Core Parameter Extraction (Basic Adjustments)** - TestExtractExposure: Verify crs:Exposure2012 → Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0) - TestExtractContrast: Verify crs:Contrast2012 → Contrast (-100 to +100) - TestExtractHighlights: Verify crs:Highlights2012 extraction (-100 to +100) - TestExtractShadows: Verify crs:Shadows2012 extraction (-100 to +100) - TestExtractWhites: Verify crs:Whites2012 extraction (-100 to +100) - TestExtractBlacks: Verify crs:Blacks2012 extraction (-100 to +100)
**FR-3: Color Parameter Extraction** - TestExtractSaturation: Verify Saturation extraction - TestExtractVibrance: Verify Vibrance extraction - TestExtractClarity: Verify Clarity2012 extraction - TestExtractSharpness: Verify Sharpness extraction - TestExtractTemperature: Verify Temperature extraction - TestExtractTint: Verify Tint extraction
**FR-4: HSL Color Adjustments Extraction** - TestExtractHSLRed: Verify Red Hue, Saturation, Luminance (-100 to +100 each) - TestExtractHSLOrange: Verify Orange HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLYellow: Verify Yellow HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLGreen: Verify Green HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLAqua: Verify Aqua HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLBlue: Verify Blue HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLPurple: Verify Purple HSL adjustments - TestExtractHSLMagenta: Verify Magenta HSL adjustments - TestExtractAllHSLColors: Verify all 8 colors × 3 properties = 24 HSL parameters
**FR-5: Advanced Features Extraction** - TestExtractToneCurve: Verify ToneCurve array of Point{X, Y} coordinates - TestExtractToneCurveEmpty: Handle files without tone curves (zero value) - TestExtractSplitToningShadowHue: Verify shadow hue extraction - TestExtractSplitToningShadowSaturation: Verify shadow saturation extraction - TestExtractSplitToningHighlightHue: Verify highlight hue extraction - TestExtractSplitToningHighlightSaturation: Verify highlight saturation extraction
**FR-6: Data Type Handling** - TestParseFloat64: Verify crs:Exposure2012="1.5" → float64(1.5) - TestParseInteger: Verify crs:Contrast2012="50" → int(50) - TestParseQuotedStrings: Handle XML attribute quoting correctly - TestParseMissingOptionalFields: Verify zero values for missing fields (no crashes) - TestParseOutOfRangeExposure: Reject Exposure > 5.0 or < -5.0 - TestParseOutOfRangeContrast: Reject Contrast > 100 or < -100 - TestParseNegativeValues: Correctly parse negative values (e.g., Shadows=-50)
**FR-7: Error Handling** - TestParseInvalidXMLSyntax: Clear error for malformed XML - TestParseMissingRequiredNamespace: Specific error message for each missing namespace - TestParseInvalidParameterValue: Error for non-numeric value in numeric field - TestParseExposureOutOfRange: Descriptive error with context - TestErrorIncludesFilePosition: Verify XML position info in error (if possible)
**NFR-1: Performance** - BenchmarkParseSingleFile: Verify <30ms per file - BenchmarkParseAllSampleFiles: Average parse time across all 913 files - BenchmarkParseComplexFile: Verify performance with HSL+ToneCurve+SplitToning
**NFR-2: Test Coverage** - TestParseAll913SampleFiles: Parse all 913 files without errors (main validation) - TestCoverageReport: Verify ≥90% coverage via `go test -cover` - TestParameterRangeValidation: Validate all extracted values within expected ranges
**NFR-3: Code Quality** - TestGoFmt: Verify `gofmt` produces no changes - TestGoVet: Verify `go vet` produces no warnings - TestBuildRecipeBuilder: Verify builder.WithExposure(), WithContrast(), etc. work correctly - TestZeroDependencies: Verify only stdlib packages imported (encoding/xml, fmt, strconv, strings)
**Edge Cases and Robustness:** - TestParseEmptyXMPFile: Minimal valid XMP with no adjustments - TestParseOnlyBasicAdjustments: File with only Exposure/Contrast - TestParseOnlyHSLAdjustments: File with only HSL colors - TestParseOnlyToneCurve: File with only tone curve - TestParseAllParametersMaximized: All parameters at maximum values - TestParseAllParametersMinimized: All parameters at minimum values - TestParseUTF8Names: Handle UTF-8 characters in preset names (if applicable) - TestParseWindowsLineEndings: Handle CRLF line endings - TestParseUnixLineEndings: Handle LF line endings - TestParseNamespaceVariations: Handle different namespace prefix orders - TestParseLightroomCCVariousVersions: Validate files from different Lightroom CC versions - TestParseParameterDiversity: Verify meaningful differences extracted across different presets
**Reference Implementation Tests (from NP3):** - Follow TestParse pattern: filepath.Glob + t.Run subtests - Follow TestParseInvalid* pattern for error handling - Follow TestValidateParametersEdgeCases pattern for boundary testing - Add TestParameterDiversity to ensure meaningful extraction (not all identical)
